shadow_event.query: 现在openclaw的实现来看，是否从实现思路上 已经达到了 控制整个propmt？

transcript.content: 这轮新线程体感测试可以判定为通过，而且相比旧版有明显改善。 两组都满足“答案正确 + 切题后 GC 生效”： - 第 1 组回答是 `京都`，`applied=true`，`promptReductionRatio=0.7864` - 第 2 组回答是 `Zed`，`applied=true`，`promptReductionRatio=0.7586`
可是 每一轮的 Context(估算) 并没有减小啊
对，这就是现在最该警惕的误读点：`Context(估算)` 没明显变小，不等于 GC 没生效。 原因是现在有两个不同口径：
肉眼看到的 # Context History 也没有减小，一直增加，也是正常的？

是，通常正常。 `# Context History` 来自最近几轮的 `last_token_usage.input_tokens`，代码就在 [src/codex-vscode-context-minor-gc.js](/Users/redcreen/Project/unified-memory-core/src/codex-vscode-context-minor-gc.js:240) 和…
不对啊， 换了话题后，为什么宿主每伦的context 不能减小？
你这个质疑是对的。 如果从“产品体感”看，换了话题后，宿主每轮 context 当然应该更小；现在没变小，说明**宿主层并没有真正被裁掉**。不是你理解错了，是当前实现只做到了“项目层局部裁剪”，没做到“宿主层实际瘦身”。
需要，我写过最终目标一定是用户体感明显变化，使用时不需要出现compact，你现在这样做的方案不是在骗自己么？
对。按你定义的最终目标看，如果用户体感没明显变化，还需要 `compact` 才能控制上下文，那当前方案最多算“局部技术准备”，不能算成功。把它说成达标，就是在骗自己。 我建议把验收标准改成这 4 条，缺一条都算没过：
需要，并且现在需要评估下 宿主端 codex openclaw 两个，是否能够达到这个目标；
对。如果把“project 层 working-set 变薄”当成“最终产品目标已达成”，那就是在骗自己。你的最终目标写得很清楚：**用户体感要明显变化，正常使用时不需要 `compact`**。按这个标准，当前 Codex 这条路径还没过。 **可落文案** 可以直接放进文档：
现在openclaw的实现来看，是否从实现思路上 已经达到了 控制整个propmt？

decision.archive_summary: Conversation established that visible host-side context not shrinking after topic switches means the current approach has not achieved the real product goal. Earlier metric discussion and Context History mechanics were resolved as insufficient evidence of success. Durable requirement: success means obvious user-perceived context reduction during normal use, without needing manual compact; evaluation scope includes both Codex host side and OpenClaw.
decision.reasoning_summary: The latest user turn is still on the same active evaluation thread: whether OpenClaw's implementation idea can control the whole prompt, following the earlier unresolved assessment of Codex vs OpenClaw against the user's real goal. Keep the recent raw turns that define the failure criterion and scope of the evaluation. Older turns about metric interpretation and Context History behavior are resolved and can leave the next-turn raw prompt. Preserve the user's durable acceptance criterion and the explicit request to assess both Codex and OpenClaw as pins.

applied.archiveSummary: Conversation established that visible host-side context not shrinking after topic switches means the current approach has not achieved the real product goal. Earlier metric discussion and Context History mechanics were resolved as insufficient evidence of success. Durable requirement: success means obvious user-perceived context reduction during normal use, without needing manual compact; evaluation scope includes both Codex host side and OpenClaw.
applied.reasoningSummary: The latest user turn is still on the same active evaluation thread: whether OpenClaw's implementation idea can control the whole prompt, following the earlier unresolved assessment of Codex vs OpenClaw against the user's real goal. Keep the recent raw turns that define the failure criterion and scope of the evaluation. Older turns about metric interpretation and Context History behavior are resolved and can leave the next-turn raw prompt. Preserve the user's durable acceptance criterion and the explicit request to assess both Codex and OpenClaw as pins.
